observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer undertake periodic reconciliation of balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts per books of accounts with that of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies to come up with accurate and reconciled RPT and SET Receivable account balances; and,
Recognize the CY 2024 RPT Receivables and consistently accrue the same in future periods.

Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant admitted that he inadvertently failed to book up the receivables at the beginning of the CY 2024. The recommendation was duly noted and the management agreed with the recommendations presented.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Management:
Ensure that the Municipal Accountant make an adjusting entry to properly classify the account;
Remit the 20 per cent share of WMSU – Main; and/or
Make a representation with WMSU – Main to reach a formal agreement to resolve issues about the WMSU-ESU lot and structures.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure:
Preparation of the necessary adjustment entries to correct the stated deficiencies; and
Moving forward, that transactions recorded in the Other MOOE account are only those that do not fall under any specific maintenance and other operating expenses account.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that LCE ensure that the:
Municipal Development Council and MEO:
Execute a MOA between the LGU and the recipient barangay setting out the terms of the transfer and requiring liquidation thereof; and
Install controls and properly monitor the implementation of development projects delegated to the recipient-barangays in accordance with DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 2020-1.
Municipal Accountant prepare correcting journal entries on the said fund transfers by crediting Due from LGUs and debiting Prior Period Adjustment and to demand the liquidation reports with complete required supporting documents from the recipient barangays.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Treasurer:
Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of stale checks which were not yet encashed at the bank and require the return of these checks for replacement;
Notify in writing the payees of outstanding checks at least one month before these become stale; and
Present in the Report of Checks Issued the cancelled checks and the replacements, if any.
We further recommended requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting journal entry to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA and Accounts Payable accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Ensure that the Municipal Accountant make representation with the ITWD to reconcile both agencies’ records as basis for any subsequent adjusting/correcting entries;
Evaluate the originating journal entry to recognize the Loans Receivable – GOCCs in the books of the LGU to determine the appropriate adjusting/correcting.

Recommendation
We recommended that the LCE ensure that:
The Municipal Accountant remind and require all AOs to liquidate their cash advances immediately;
Institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, or by withholding/suspension of the salaries of active AOs;
The Municipal Accountant comply with the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2016 - 005 dated December 19, 2016 which provides for the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies, LGUs and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations.

Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant commented that he will no longer allow cash advances to officers and employees who have an outstanding cash advance. Overall, the Management concurred and agreed to comply with the recommendations presented.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Take into consideration the imposition of liquidated damages for those projects that meets the conditions as specified in Item 9.1, Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 (Updated as of 15 April 2023);
Strictly follow and monitor the project timeliness and ensure prompt implementation of the projects. Perform an inspection to ascertain the current status of the project, assess and identify the magnitude of any damage or loss, take necessary measures to ensure the prompt completion of the project, in order for the intended recipients to promptly benefit from the projects; and
Ensure and monitor the Engineering Office to expedite the immediate resolution of the issues encountered at the two infrastructure projects noted to be in delay.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the Legislative Department to immediately determine and approve of the amount of rental fee for each unit.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact and within the reglementary period to the LGU’s AGDB all collections of the local treasury to safeguard funds from loss and/or misuse, misapplication or misappropriation.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to monitor that the LSB streamline the reporting and monitoring process of the SEF Budget by ensuring that the LSB furnish a copy of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization to the Sanggunian Bayan and DBM.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the unliquidated fund transfer, liquidate the funds and/or return any remaining balance to the DOT for proper accounting and reporting.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant already coordinated with the Accountant of DOT. He also added the corresponding SORD has already submitted.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE ensure that the Inventory Committee:
Expedite the disposal process for unserviceable assets to prevent further deterioration and decrease of value; and
Conduct periodic assessments and monitoring of properties to improve its property and supply management by timely disposing any unserviceable properties in accordance with Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties and other pertinent laws, rules, and regulations.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Withhold five per cent creditable taxes based on gross income for the honoraria/professional fees rendered as prescribed in the RR No. 11-2018; and
File and remit the same withheld taxes to the BIR within the due date.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management through the BAC Chairman agreed, to ensure that the BAC Secretariat send written notice to losing bidders notifying the bidding results and the recommendation of award of the contract.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the BAC to furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the perfected contracts together with the supporting documents relative to the above – mentioned procurement of government projects for COA technical review and inspection.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that:
Strategies, in coordination with the different heads of offices, be formulated to expedite the processing of the DVs, payrolls and RCDs;
The Municipal Accountant submit immediately the transaction documents such as DVs, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc., and their corresponding supporting documents, and henceforth, submit it within the reglementary period to ensure their timely evaluation and that deficiencies and/or other observations will be also immediately corrected and/or adjusted; and
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that personnel in the Accounting Office particularly those involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts to turn over the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within 10 days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management:
Create/assign a separate RC and RC Code for the GFPS to account, monitor and report GAD expenses and other GAD-related financial transactions; and
Monitor and ensure that the Municipal Accountant to maintain an SL for GAD for each account with the corresponding SL code if it is impractical to establish separate RC for GAD.

Management Comment
The Management agreed to prepare SL for GAD in lieu of the RC for GAD.
a.    Require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately;

We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to exert extra effort to trace the unidentified beginning balances or through confirmation of balances from claimants/person responsible, verify the supporting documents to establish its validity, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts, and so as not to defeat the purpose of the FS in providing vital information to its users; and onwards, observe proper recording of transactions with complete supporting documents, and maintain respective records that in turn will support the existence and correct valuation of accounts in the FS. For receivable accounts, if warranted and duly justified, request for write off of accounts may be made. Emphasis should be made on the completeness of the account schedules and/or subsidiary ledgers which serves as basis in the transition of manual to electronic accounting system.

1. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Accountant at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. Also, at year-end to certify delinquencies remaining uncollected for all RPT delinquencies.

2. Municipal Assessor to furnish Local Treasurer with Assessment Roll on or before thirty first day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of each properties.

3.  Accountant shall draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivables/ Special Education Tax Receivable and crediting to Deferred Real Property Tax/ Special Education Tax Income with corresponding schedule.

4. Municipal Treasurer submitted Certified List of Delinquencies Remaining Uncollected as of December 31 to be reconciled with the Municipal Accountant’s Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivables at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to consider the asset recognition principle in recording procurements made by the municipality. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to properly reclassify the accounts to come up with a more reliable financial statement. The recording of expenditures shall only be made upon issuance of supplies and materials as provided in Section 121 of the NGAS Manual. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for recording of inventories as described in sections 175-207.

2.    The Inventory Committee conduct inventory taking activities according to previously approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count, using the prescribed format under the Accounting Manual and submit the same not later than January 31 of the ensuing; Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs volume II to follow the prescribed forms and reports described in sections 61 to 74 as illustrated in Appendices 54 to 67 of the manual. And to submit the same in accordance with GAM for LGUs volume I, section 266 not later than January 31 of the ensuing year.

3.    The Property Unit and Accounting Unit undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled; Moving forward to the implementation of the GAM for LGU follow the pertinent rules as described in volume 1, section 269; and

4.    The management undertake disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs and derecognition procedures of non-existing/missing PPE without available record of accountability as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to ensure proper classification of assets in the books;
Make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts.
Provide data to the Accounting Office relevant to items reclassified as semi-expendable property including information of its condition.

Update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/ registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular; and
Onwards, use the ICS for issuance of semi-expandable items to establish monitoring, control and accountability over them.

a.    The concerned offices to prioritize the titling of the land to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest and ensure that its land are free from legal issues before facilities are constructed thereon and for the GSO/Legal Office to maintain a record/custody of all the legal documents pertaining to the properties acquired, whether by cash or donation.
b.    The Accounting Office to coordinate with the GSO and Assessor’s office to provide reconciliation amongst their records.

c.     Municipal Accountant derecognized the donated lot in the PPE Account representing transfer of ownership.  A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) should be prepared by the accounting unit and furnished a copy to COA for reference
a.     The Inventory Committee to identify land/real properties owned by the Municipality and require the appraisal of the lots by a licensed appraiser for purposes of the land valuation; and
b. The Accountant to consider application of the Revaluation Model as accounting policy on the Land account of the PPE, to come up with a fair presentation of the account balance.

a.    Municipal Accountant perform a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts and prepare the necessary correcting journal entry vouchers to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements and to prepare and maintain CIP Ledger Cards (CIPLCs) on a per project basis and to effect the transfer of completed projects from the CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts and ensure that proper accounts are used in recording transactions;
b.    Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accounting Office with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents;
c.     Municipal Treasurer to update and reconcile inventory records; and

d.    Inventory committee created thru an issuance of a written order complete the actual physical count on a specified time frame and submit complete physical inventory report in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual.

1.    The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road Network; and

2.    The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account, and to properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist be directed to conduct the physical inventory of the Biological Asset. The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account to the proper asset account and to reconcile with the records of the Municipal Treasurer. Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Biological Assets transactions are to be in accordance with Sections 304 to 315.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to formulate an accounting policy for the useful life and accounting estimates for abovementioned Intangible Assets. Further, to record and adjust the unrecorded current and prior years’ amortization expenses. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to strictly follow required disclosures and for the Amortization of Intangible Assets as described in sections 297 and 302 of Volume I.

a.    Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the liquidation report and return the savings from completed projects to the source agencies. Henceforth, projects should be fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation;
b.    Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of on-going projects; and

c.     Municipal Accountant reconcile the amounts between the controlling account, the General Ledger (GL), and the SL, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts.

1.    Municipal Accountant to determine the valid claimants of the balances indicated and prepare subsidiary ledgers for each valid claimant. Balances with no valid claimants should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and should be subject for appropriation in the next immediate budget period.
2.    Municipal Accountant to identify the reasons for the non-remittance of funds due to the Provincial Government and POPCOM and take up the necessary adjustments, as necessary;
1.    Create a RC for the GAD Focal Point System; and
2.    Regularly visit the COA website to be updated with the all the recent COA issuances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated financial statements. Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, future restatements are to be in accordance with Sections 246 and 247.

We recommended that Management Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference between the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the amount in the Statement of Financial Performance. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for SCBAA as described in sections 370 – 373 of Volume I.

2. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.

3.  Each office of the LGU to fill up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper.
4. Inventory Committee to record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.

5. Property Unit to  prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of year the list of all the on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of the PPAs. The prescribed form provided in the circular shall be used indicating in the remarks column the mode of procurement and specific status of the project such as on-going, for implementation, completed, suspended/ cancelled, terminated and for bidding.

The GSO justified that PPE acquired are insured immediately after its delivery that resulted to different period of renewal for insurable PPEs. Also, they will observe the proper PPE items that are insurable.
1. Sanggunian, Municipal Budget Officer and other responsible offices to prepare and submit Annual Operating Budget (AOB) for each LEEs that is supported by a BP and SAFSSR;
2.  Sanggunian, LFC or Economic Development Officer if any, to deliberate and prepare a 5-year Business Plan (BP) especially with the newly established LEEs;
3.  LFC to conduct semi-annual review and general examination of cost and accomplishments against performance standards.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the gaps presented.
1.  Correct the internal issues presented by the computer system, and make a patch to improve the already existing system; and

2. Request that continuous customer support be easily available whenever issues were presented; If the updating of SL feature is not yet available, then the provider must incorporate it in the system since it is indispensable in an accounting system.

1.   conducting management audit, and the evaluation of the results of operations, focusing on the effectiveness of controls of operating systems and support services/units/systems and documenting the same; and
2. preparing the various reports and documents presented in the manual.

We recommended that the management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildingsowned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned officials/employees and the SWM Board the preparation of an annual SWM accomplishment/progress report on the strategies implemented at the end of the year for submission to the NSWC copy furnished the Audit Team.

We recommended that management intensify its effort to have an approved 10-year Ecological SWMP by NSWMC to ensure efficient management of solid waste and enhancing ecological balance purposely for the general welfare of the entire constituents.

We recommended that the municipality close the dump site or convert it to an eco-park to avoid health and environmental hazards and to avoid penal sanctions as provided by RA 9003. Further, the municipality make provisions for the development and operation of a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of the municipality and to expeditethe coordination with the DENR to facilitate the conversion of the dump site into a sanitary landfill.
We recommended that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places.

Further, We recommended that the ordinance include provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the LCE instructs the GFPS to include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit.
Further, We recommended that the GFPS conduct gender audit on its agency through the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.

We recommended that the LGU create and establish Monitoring and Evaluation Team in accordance with the provision Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct and ensure:
The concerned offices to prioritize the titling of the land to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest and ensure that its land is free from legal issues before facilities are constructed thereon and for the GSO/Legal Office to maintain a record/custody of all the legal documents pertaining to the properties acquired, whether by cash or donation; and
The Accounting Office to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor’s office to provide reconciliation amongst their records.
Recommendation/s:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate each other in identifying these PPEs and to depreciate them accordingly. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to record and adjust the unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expenses.
Moving forward, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to implement the guidelines and procedures as prescribe in COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to clean up these PPE accounts. Likewise, moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for Depreciation of PPE as described in sections 260 – 262.
Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct:
The Inventory Committee to:
Conduct actual physical count of the road network system per books;
Update the Physical Count Report on the Local Road Network and forward the report to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation, copy furnished COA.
The GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain and regularly update the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
The Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components;
Reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and
Properly disclose the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements as at year-end in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct:
The Municipal Accountant to apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to ensure proper classification of assets in the books and make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts.
The GSO Designate or the Municipal Treasurer to provide data to the Accounting Office relevant to items reclassified as semi-expendable property including information of its condition.  To update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular; and onwards, to use the ICS for issuance of semi-expandable items to establish monitoring, control and accountability over them.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant with coordination of Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to:
Collect these long outstanding receivables particularly the accounts with available reference documents by sending demand letters. Exhaust all remedies to collect and properly document efforts made;
Locate all related documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivables in order to establish their existence and validity;
File a request for write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008, if warranted, but continue to exert effort to collect the written-off accounts; and (ii) develop policies and guidelines outlining the methods and strategies to improve the collection and settlement of past due accounts and thereafter ensure strict adherence thereto to cleanse the books for the fair presentation of receivable accounts in the Financial Statements; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) develop accounting estimate for impairment of receivables based on the management’s knowledge and experience on the settlement of its receivables taking into account the age and collectability of the balances, identified bad accounts eligible for write-off and other historical data such as settlement/liquidation and collection experience; (ii) assess at the end of each year or each reporting date whether there is evidence that the receivable accounts are impaired; (iii) support the assessment, if possible, with evidence enumerated in paragraph 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; (iv) calculate the allowance for impairment in accordance with the IPSAS and recognize the computed allowance in the books to reflect the net realizable value of the receivable accounts in the Financial Statements; and (v) submit to the Local Chief Executive the recommended list of eligible accounts for write-off.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. Also, at year-end to certify delinquencies remaining uncollected for all RPT delinquencies.
Municipal Assessor to furnish Local Treasurer with Assessment Roll on or before thirty first day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of each property.
Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivables/ Special Education Tax Receivable and crediting to Deferred Real Property Tax/ Special Education Tax Income with corresponding schedule.
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective Certified List of Delinquencies Remaining Uncollected as of December 31 and the Municipal Accountant’s Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivables at year-end.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Agriculturist to conduct the physical inventory of the Biological Assets and properly identify the subsequent value of the assets based on IPSAS 27 and to reconcile with the records of the Municipal Treasurer;
Municipal Accountant:
Adjust the asset account based on the revalued amount; and
Record all subsequent purchases/acquisition of biological assets in accordance with IPSAS 27.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the liquidation report and return the savings from completed projects to the source agencies. Henceforth, projects should be fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation;
Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of on-going projects; and
Municipal Accountant reconcile the amounts between the controlling account, the General Ledger (GL), and the SL, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts.

Recommendations:

Utilize the correct liability account to report all transfers from PhilHealth in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
Retrieve appropriate documents and check actual liquidation of funds made to PhilHealth and if possible, reconcile records with the Due from LGU account on the PhilHealth books;
Perform a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts and prepare the necessary correcting journal entry vouchers to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and
Strengthen accounting controls by performing a regular review on the journal entry and posting in the ledgers made by the accounting staff.

Recommendations:

Also, Management to instruct the HRMO is regularly update the amount of Terminal Leave Benefits earned by retired/separated employees even though the benefits remained to be unclaimed for various reasons.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the implementation of development projects delegated to the recipient-barangays are properly monitored to ensure regularity by DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 2020-1. To facilitate the fund transfers made, a Memorandum of Agreement between parties be made; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting journal entries on the said fund transfers by crediting Due from Local Government Units and debiting Prior Period Adjustment and to demand the liquidation reports with complete required supporting documents from the recipient barangays.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly observe the standards on the preparation of the financial statements by restating the next set of Financial Statements and disclosing the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
Revisit the IPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Properly disclose the implementation of the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, and financial and non-financial effects to the LGU;
Revise the account name of the account “Share from Internal Revenue Allotment” to “Share from National Tax Allotment” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and
Moving forward, to study constantly IFAC publications vis-a-vis COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team about the proper implementation of the same whenever possible.
Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements.
Revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 2 and other COA issuances, particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the FS. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and
Municipal Budget Officer to provide the necessary information needed by the Municipal Accountant in preparing the Reconciliation between Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and Statement of Financial Performance.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a special account in the general fund for the assets, liabilities and operating performance of the various local economic enterprises; and
Instruct the LFC to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the proper reporting and monitoring of the revenue and expense of the respective LEEs concerned.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor to:
Proactively coordinate with the OPA to allow immediate scheduling and/or finalization of timelines on the planned province-wide assessment; and
Plan and implement the expected update of the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct and assure that the Human Resources Management Officer (HRMO) hasten the recruitment and selection process of the remaining unfilled positions.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to communicate with pertinent government agencies and request their performance indicators and incorporate it in their monitoring and evaluation functions and to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to set up the appropriate mechanism to collect comparable data and information on a regular basis and provide timely analysis of the information.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the provider of the Accounting System and request the:
Performance of the appropriate trouble shooting procedures to correct the identified internal system issues and glitches in the Accounting System ensuring that the system contains all the necessary features and operates efficiently and as intended; and,
Continuous customer support be easily available whenever issues are presented.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the remaining gaps presented.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the IAS Officer to timely conduct an evaluation or appraisal of the ICS to determine if internal controls are well designed and properly implemented as required by the PGIAM by:
Appraising the adequacy of internal controls and documenting the same;
Conducting management audit, and the evaluation of the results of operations, focusing on the effectiveness of controls of operating systems and support services/units/systems and documenting the same; and
Preparing the various reports and documents presented in the manual.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to implement it’s 10-year SWMP for CY 2019 – 2029 consistent with the National SWM framework and the NSWMC guidebook especially on the estimated income and expected operating and capital expenditures. Further make sure that the 10-year SWMP is approved by the NSWMC.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to institutionalize the APCPI by instructing the BAC Secretariat to review the user manual for the preparation of the APCPI and to timely prepare and submit the report and prepare the 2021 to 2023 APCPI and prospectively the results for the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of year the list of all the on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the quarterly monitoring report on the implementation of the PPAs of all funds. We recommended that the management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned officials/employees and the SWM Board the preparation of an annual SWM accomplishment/progress report on the strategies implemented at the end of the year for submission to the NSWC copy furnished the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the respective heads of offices to present their respective Citizen’s Charter in a clear, readable, concise and engaging for the citizen or client who are transacting with them through tarpaulins and/or other display modalities.

We recommended that the LCE thru HRMO to create policy guidelines in the hiring of personnel under job order/contract of services in accordance with CSC, Revised Omnibus Rules, COA and other existing labor laws, rules and regulations, to give the workers, both regular and casual/contractual/job order, the benefits due them, define their accountabilities and contribute to the overall productivity of the Municipality Government.
The LCE to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipality HRMO and Municipality Budget must set plans and come up with an Organizational Structure such that all vacant positions be filled-up instead of resorting to Job Order/Contact of Service scheme and must include in their personnel schedule those appointed as co-terminus, contractual and contract of service.
Further, if merited by applicable CSC regulations, HRMO to evaluate various positions Job Order/Contract of Service personnel for possible appointment to permanent regular positions.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the LGU Accountant and other personnel responsible in determining the income payments of personnel engaged under job order or contract of services arrangement to properly comply with tax laws and other applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management:
Initiate the needed coordination, monitoring and evaluation on the implementation to assure that all unimplemented projects and programs be given immediate action and implementation. Such that, the execution of the respective responsibilities of the Local Development Council, Executive Committee and of the Local Chief Executive as mandated must be strictly adhered.
Faithfully adhere to the policies and procedures in coming up with a supplemental budget or for any changes in the approved Annual Budget, especially for special funds like the 20% DF, by proper evaluation of the status of PPAs, which will be the basis in reverting programs and projects or to establish savings therefrom as source of appropriation; and
Onwards, require the LGU Planning and Development Office to submit a yearly cumulative status of the PPAs, specifically under the 20% DF to facilitate monitoring and evaluation thereof.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize the APCPI by instructing the BAC Secretariat to study the user manual for the preparation of the APCPI
observations.managementAction: and to prepare the 2021 and 2022 APCPI results.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Management to adopt and maintain a CBMS database for use in local level planning and program implementation;
Local Sanggunian to come up with appropriation ordinances for the project implementation and /or include in the ensuing years Annual Budget; and
Local Chief Executive to implement and supervise the implementation of RA 11315.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to provide adequate and available resources for the collection and processing of data requirements of the GAD Database. Also, we recommend that the LCE instruct the GFPS to:
Create and institutionalize the GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data to provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation to effectively plan and implement GAD-related PAPs;
Coordinate with the Planning Department in the updating of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
Ensure that the gender issues contained in the ensuing GPB are based from the results of gender analysis conducted on the GAD Database and/or any other gender analysis tools.

We recommended that Management ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the prudent utilization of the GAD Budget in compliance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 and 2016-01.

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant the following:
Observe the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 particularly the transfer of unspent allotment of Current Year LDRMM Fund to special trust fund;
Carefully monitor the balances per ledgers, schedules and reports and reconcile the balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM with the Cash-in Bank 5% Calamity fund and other related accounts once the actual cash was transferred by the MTO to the LDRRM bank account to establish a sufficient cash back-up for the said trust account. We also require the reconciliation amongst accounts. A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) should be prepared in recording the adjusting/correcting entries and furnished a copy of which to COA for reference; and
Prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to consider the asset recognition principle in recording procurements made by the municipality. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to properly reclassify the accounts to come up with a more reliable financial statement. The recording of expenditures shall only be made upon issuance of supplies and materials as provided in Section 121 of the NGAS Manual. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for recording of inventories as described in sections 175-207.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the processing of documents needed to support the receipt of the donated property from the PLGU.  For the recorded properties without cost, to use the fair market value of the motor vehicle during the time of donation, and to coordinate with Rural Transit Mindanao Incorporated and request for the net book value of the bus upon time of donation. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize the transfer of assets from the PLGU Sibugay in the books of accounts of the LGU and to record the cost of the remaining vehicles already recorded without any cost. Further, depreciate retrospectively the PPE from the moment of receipt of the assets and not during recording of the assets.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate each other in identifying these PPEs and to depreciate them accordingly. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to record and adjust the unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expenses.
Moving forward, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to implement the guidelines and procedures as prescribe in COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to clean up these PPE accounts. Likewise, moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for Depreciation of PPE as described in sections 260 – 262.

We recommended that:

The Head of the Agency create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The committee shall also have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units;
The Inventory Committee conduct inventory taking activities according to previously approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count, using the prescribed format under the Accounting Manual and submit the same not later than January 31 of the ensuing; Moving forward to the implementation of  the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs volume II to follow the prescribed forms and reports described in sections 61 to 74 as illustrated in Appendices 54 to 67 of the manual. And to submit the same in accordance with GAM for LGUs volume I, section 266 not later than January 31 of the ensuing year.
The Property Unit and Accounting Unit undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled; Moving forward to the implementation of the GAM for LGU follow the pertinent rules as described in volume 1, section 269; and
The management undertake disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs and derecognition procedures of non-existing/missing PPE without available record of accountability as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that:

The HRMO and Accounting Office submit the required documents as enumerated above to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of subject transaction and that onwards, claims should be duly processed and supported with complete documents before it should be paid, to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in audit. 

The Accounting Office to stop the practice of processing transactions that have incomplete documents.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify & record necessary adjusting entries in order to present the financial statements fairly in accordance with the IPSAS 1.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation; and 
Require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the outstanding checks if there are any stale checks and send written notice to the payee of the existence of such check.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to have a legal expert to study the legality of Sanguniang Panlalawigan and Sanguniang Bayan Ordinances issued by their respective Sanguniang Members before implementing it in the LGU in order to prevent future misrepresentations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to orient the various revenue collectors on the Local Government Code, the Local Treasury Manual and the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in order for them to be knowledgeable of the various laws, rules and regulations concerning revenue collection. Further, include in the next information drive of the Municipality the need of large cattle owners to register their large cattle with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and require the registration of large cattle upon reaching the age of two (2) years, as well as the registration of sale of transfer of ownership of large cattle

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer be directed to prepare and submit the applicable reports in the Microsoft (MS) Excel format relevant to their receipts, transfers and utilizations of funds using the templates prescribed by COA Circular 2021 - 02. Further, for Management to regularly visit the COA Website to be informed of any updated COA Circulars which affects the financial transactions and operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with supporting documents to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), copy furnished the Audit Team for all donations received, COVID-19 or non-COVID-19 related supplies and equipment from the private companies in calendar year 2021 and every quarter thereafter, if there is any. Further, for Management to regularly visit the COA Website to be informed of any updated COA Circulars which affects the financial transactions and operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire an IAS Officer, and instruct the IAS Officer to strictly adhere and harmonize its activities to the provisions of AO No. 278 dated April 28, 1992, and the PGIAM to perform its mandated functions.

We recommended that the LGU comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF as required by DepEd, DBM, and DILG JC No. 01. Also, the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to timely prepare and submit all the required reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer / Cashier / Accountant submit the transaction documents: Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents, within the reglementary period to ensure their timely evaluation and that deficiencies and/or other observations will be also immediately corrected and/or adjusted.

The personnel involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts shall turnover the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that onwards:

The Municipality of Ipil to enact Supplemental Budget ordinance for funds received without specific purpose and to send a copy thereof to contributor LGU to facilitate monitoring of funds transferred.

Further, it is recommended that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to send a written notice to the payees of the stale checks informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed. We recommended that Management submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and provide the required signboards in conspicuous places within the agency premises and at the project site of all infrastructure projects pursuant to the policy on full disclosure as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004.

3.13	We recommended that management:

Strictly enforce collection strategies to avoid accumulation of uncollected stall rentals such as non-renewal of business license for failure to pay back rentals; and
Send demand letters to these delinquent stall occupants compelling them to immediately settle their balances.

Recommendation
4.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 59 of LGU-NGAS Volume I Chapter 3 on Spoiled and Stale Checks and record adjustments for valid reconciling items. Further, it is recommended that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to send a written notice to the payees of the stale checks informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed. If the checks could not be retrieved because the same has been issued years back, we recommend to adjust the cash in bank account by restoring the balance of cash in bank and setting up the applicable accounts payable duly supported with a copy of the BRS. Furthermore, the Journal Entry Voucher should be adequately described and duly supported with a copy of the Disbursement Voucher and the stale check.

Recommendation
6.9	We recommended that appropriate adjusting entries be made to correct the noted misclassification of the account.  Furthermore, the AOMs by COA ICFAU on the CIF liquidation should be acted on and complied by management.

Recommendation
7.8	We recommended that Management:

Provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.

Recommendation
8.6	We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation. See to it that those development projects identified in the AIP were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize the development projects that could be implemented during the year by taking into consideration the technical manpower of the Municipality.  Consider changes in the annual budget for projects which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

9.5	We recommended that Management direct the MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee to fast track the implementation of on-going development projects and monitor the implementation of the same so that any cause of suspension and/or problems in the course of implementation be immediately addressed.

Recommendation

Recommendation

No. 2009-001 by submitting the copies of the government contracts with all the supporting documents (certified true copy) within the period prescribed in order to avoid the aforementioned penalties.

Recommendation
12.7	We recommended that management direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) in developing the Municipality’s Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan (LDRRMP), it should consider the four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery. Recommendation

Recommendation

The Municipal Treasurer to observe prudence in the issuance of checks to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures at the same time preserving the scarce resources of the government. Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
reconcile their respective records.

a-3) Failure to establish an internal control over property and supply
management.
Pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Chapter 7 of
Handbook on Property & Supply Management System (PSMS), the Local Government
Unit is encouraged to establish Internal Control System (ICS) to prevent losses to the
organization arising from the different hazards in government operations.
The objectives of internal controls:
• To safeguard the reliability of data
• To safeguard assets and records
• To promote operational efficiency
• To encourage adherence to prescribed policies
Basic Requirements for Internal Control over PSMS are:
1. Adequate storage space properly enclosed and protected from access of
unauthorized personnel should be provided;
2. Perpetual inventory records should be kept by an individual other than
storekeeper;
3. Purchased equipment and supplies should be properly tagged; supplies should
be properly tagged and identified through bin cards; and
4. Employees should be rotated on the job. Vacations for those in position of trust
should be enforced for check and balance and generation of innovative ideas.
In our audit observation, equipment and supplies were not properly tagged for
identification and inventory purposes.
Recommendation:
We recommend that the management should establish internal control policies and
procedures over Property and Supply Management system to:
a. Safeguard the accuracy and reliability of the information by preventing and
detecting errors;
b. Prevent or detect loss of assets and records on a timely basis;
c. Protect against waste in all aspects of operation and discourage other types of
inefficient use of resources; and
d. Provide reasonable assurance that those procedures and rules are adhered to by
the agency personnel.

b) No adequate storage and warehouse facilities of supplies, several
vehicles and heavy equipment.
The purpose of constructing proper storage and warehousing facilities is for the best
safekeeping of the materials and equipment. Best safekeeping means protecting the
materials against theft, fire and deterioration but easily accessible when needed.
The responsibility to take care that such policy is faithfully adhered rest directly
with the chief or head of the government agency concerned.
In the course of our audit, we observed that several vehicles of the Municipality
were left in an unsecured area. Sangguniang Resolution No. 07-395-2017 dated July
12,2017 effectively donated a portion parcel of land identified as lot no. 4053-B-2, Psd-09-
025310 containing an area of 217 sq.m. for the construction of the new building of
Municipal Police Station thus the current motor pool area of the LGU for parking of heavy
equipment significantly reduced.
Recommendation:
We recommend that the management to provide adequate and safe shelter for
storage of supplies, parking space of the vehicle/equipment to safeguard and to prolong the
useful life of the assets.
Management Comment:
The management committed to discuss the matter with the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer which is assigned as property custodian.
As of now, they have not provided proper storage of the properties due to the fact
that LGU Ipil is in the process of constructing a new municipal hall which includes the
construction of proper storage for municipal properties.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant has already reconciled only the Trial
Balance because no reports have been properly made regarding properties yet.
The management committed to comply with the recommendations stated here as
soon as possible.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete set of financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records with those of the Treasurer’s Office to determine what items were disposed of and were not covered with approved IIRUP. The preparation and approval of the IIRUP shall be the basis for the Municipal Accountant in dropping from the books of accounts, the disposed unserviceable properties.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support their daily needs until the next payday.

We recommend that  management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification in compliance with the above cited regulation.

We recommend that the management to observe the following:
Submission of contracts, purchase order, loan agreements and other agreements entered into by the LGU to the auditor within five (5) days upon perfection thereof pursuant to Section 6.6 of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-002; and
Notify the auditor of deliveries of goods and services procured by the agency within twenty four (24) hours after acceptance pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommend that official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. The Monthly reports will be the summary of trips per vehicles in chronological order of the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.

We advise the Municipal Accountant to examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transactions of the Municipality before approving the same. If there are claims submitted to the Accounting Office for processing, but lacks the required supporting documents, it shall be returned to the claimant for proper documentation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor to implement the use of the biometric system in monitoring the time ins and outs and attendance of the employees to achieve the intended purpose of the system.
The machine generated DTRs to form part of the supporting/documents of the payroll.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain the registries as follows:
a. 	Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, Capital Outlays (RAAOCO).
b. 	Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (RAAOMO).
c. 	Registry of Appropriations and Obligations Personal Services (RAAOPS).
d. 	Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations Financial Expenses (RAAOFE).
The format of these forms can be referred to in Annexes 13, 14, 15, 15 Volume II of the NGAS manual. At the back of these forms are instructions on how it shall be accomplished.

Require the personnel concerned in the Accounting and Budget to use the proper account codes are prescribed in the Philippine Public Sector Standards on Auditing (PPSSAs) ‘ Revised  Charts of Accounts to have a fair presentation of accounts.
As in-charge of the keeping of the books accounts the accounting personnel should update themselves with (PPSSAs)  and faithfully adhere to its implementation to ensure correct and accurate financial reports and to avoid possible suspension/s and or disallowance/s in audit.

We recommend that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in accordance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.
